fact: November 9 is exactly 313 days into the year - that's the same number forwards and backwards!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many trees are completely bald, having dropped their last stubborn leaves in the past few days.
Scorpio season is in full swing on November 9, making it one of the most mysterious and intense days of the zodiac calendar.
Why do cats get extra fluffy around this time? Their winter coats are reaching peak thickness to prepare for the coldest months ahead.
In northern regions, the sun sets before 5 PM on November 9, creating those dramatic orange and purple twilight skies that last forever.
November 9 sits exactly 42 days before winter solstice, which means daylight is shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day in most places.
Spiders are desperately trying to get inside houses around this date because outdoor temperatures are becoming too cold for them to survive.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, November 9 is when jacaranda trees explode with bright purple flowers that carpet entire streets.
The first hard frost of the year typically happens around November 9 in many temperate regions, turning morning grass into crunchy crystal carpets.
Squirrels are at their most frantic on days like this, burying up to 25 nuts per day as they race against winter's arrival.
